title: Walk through.
subtitle: Day 5
excerpt: One step. Today. The smallest one that moves the needle.

title: The Terror of History
subtitle: On Eliade, meaning, and why suffering needs a meaning.
excerpt: When suffering loses its meaning, it becomes unbearable. Eliade called it the "terror of history." Meaning has to be built, not inherited.

title: You Have Never Suffered From Anything But Love
excerpt: Every grief, every breakup, every quiet midnight: the same suffering wearing different masks. It's always love.

title: Find the doorway.
subtitle: Day 4
excerpt: The thing you've been calling a wall has been a door the whole time.

title: Sit with it.
subtitle: Day 3
excerpt: The instinct will be to fix. The work is to feel.

title: Name the spiral.
subtitle: Day 2
excerpt: You can't break what you can't see. Today's work is one sentence long.

title: Stop pushing.
subtitle: Day 1
excerpt: The first thing you have to stop doing is the thing you're proudest of.

title: The doorway hidden inside your suffering.
excerpt: Most people treat their suffering as a problem to be eliminated. What if it's actually a door — and you've been pushing on the wrong side?

title: Why meaning beats motivation
excerpt: Motivation runs out by Wednesday. Meaning shows up uninvited at 3am. Here's why one is fuel and the other is gravity.

title: The lie of "just push through"
excerpt: Sometimes the wall is information, not an obstacle. Here's how to tell which one you're actually facing.

title: What I learned in the dark
excerpt: The year I stopped pretending was the year I started becoming. Notes from the bottom.

title: How to interview your own pain
excerpt: The most useful question I know — and the silence that has to follow it.

title: Stuckness is information
excerpt: If you keep starting over, you're not lazy. You're listening to something. Here's how to hear it.

title: On grief and gravity
excerpt: Grief doesn't lift. It teaches you how to walk with weight. A slow conversation with myself about loss.
